title: centaurea ornata
intro: Centaurea ornata, also known as Golden Cockscomb or Mountain Bluet, is a flowering plant in the daisy family, characterized by its vibrant yellow or white flowers with a distinctive shape resembling a cock's comb. Traditionally, Centaurea ornata has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been reported to aid in wound healing, reducing fever, and combating infections. The plant is rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which are believed to contribute to its health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects, potentially supporting cardiovascular health and immune function. Centaurea ornata can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and it may also be used as a topical application for skin issues, making it a popular herbal remedy in traditional medicine.
